maml:description.maml:para: Disables personal workspace for a user on UiPath Orchestrator.
Disables the personal workspace feature for the specified user on UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet updates the user settings by setting the MayHavePersonalWorkspace property to False.
After disabling the personal workspace, the cmdlet automatically clears the in-memory cache to reflect the changes.

The -UserName parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available tenant users. The -UserName completions are dynamically populated from actual user data on the target drives.
This is a script function (external help file: UiPathOrch-Help.xml) that wraps the Update-OrchUser cmdlet.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users, PUT /odata/Users({userId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.View, Users.Edit
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the user name of the user to disable personal workspace for. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the user name of the user to disable personal workspace for. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
You can pipe user names to this cmdlet via the UserName property.
This cmdlet does not produce output directly. It calls Update-OrchUser to update user settings.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet sets MayHavePersonalWorkspace to False for the specified user. Unlike Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace, it does not modify the MayHaveRobotSession setting. The cache is automatically cleared after the operation.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Disable personal workspace for a user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Disable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com ```
Disables the personal workspace for the user ytsuda@gmail.com on the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Disable personal workspace on a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Disable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com -Path Orch1: ```
Disables the personal workspace for the specified user on the Orch1: drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview changes with WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Disable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually making changes.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables the attended robot session for users.

Disables the attended robot session (MayHaveRobotSession) for specified users in UiPath Orchestrator. This is a convenience wrapper around Update-OrchUser that sets -MayHaveRobotSession to False for the specified users.
When a user has MayHaveRobotSession disabled, they are not allowed to run attended automations using UiPath Assistant on their machine.
The -UserName parameter supports wildcards to disable the attended session for multiple users at once. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
The -UserName and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users, PUT /odata/Users({userId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.View, Users.Edit
Specifies the target Orch: drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Tab completion suggests available Orch: drives.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the usernames of users to disable the attended robot session for. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target Orch: drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Tab completion suggests available Orch: drives.
Specifies the usernames of users to disable the attended robot session for. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
You can pipe a single username to this cmdlet via the UserName property.
You can pipe usernames and path values to this cmdlet via the UserName and Path properties.
This cmdlet does not produce output. The user is updated via Update-OrchUser internally.

maml:alert.maml:para:
This is a PowerShell script function that wraps Update-OrchUser with -MayHaveRobotSession False. Each specified user is updated individually in a loop. The function also passes all bound parameters via splatting (@PSBoundParameters).
Users are tenant-scoped entities. Navigate to the root of an Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target drives.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Disable attended robot for a specific user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Disable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda@gmail.com ```
Disables the attended robot session for the user "ytsuda@gmail.com". The -UserName parameter is positional (position 0) so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Disable attended robot for multiple users with a wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Disable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda* ```
Disables the attended robot session for all users whose username starts with "ytsuda".

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Disable attended robot from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Disable-OrchUserAttended -Path Orch1:\ -UserName ytsuda+c@gmail.com ```
Disables the attended robot session for "ytsuda+c@gmail.com" on the Orch1 tenant. When -Path uses an absolute path, the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview changes with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Disable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda* -WhatIf ```
Shows which users would have their attended robot session disabled without actually making changes.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables personal workspace for a user on UiPath Orchestrator.

Enables the personal workspace feature for the specified user on UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet updates the user settings to allow them to have a personal workspace and robot session by setting the MayHavePersonalWorkspace and MayHaveRobotSession properties to True.
After enabling the personal workspace, the cmdlet automatically clears the in-memory cache to reflect the changes.

The -UserName parameter supports tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available tenant users. The -UserName completions are dynamically populated from actual user data on the target drives.
This is a script function (external help file: UiPathOrch-Help.xml) that wraps the Update-OrchUser cmdlet.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users, PUT /odata/Users({userId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.View, Users.Edit
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the user name of the user to enable personal workspace for. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted.
Specifies the user name of the user to enable personal workspace for. Accepts wildcard patterns.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
You can pipe user names to this cmdlet via the UserName property.
This cmdlet does not produce output directly. It calls Update-OrchUser to update user settings.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet sets both MayHavePersonalWorkspace and MayHaveRobotSession to True for the specified user. The cache is automatically cleared after the operation.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Enable personal workspace for a user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com ```
Enables the personal workspace for the user ytsuda@gmail.com on the current drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Enable personal workspace on a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com -Path Orch1: ```
Enables the personal workspace for the specified user on the Orch1: drive.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Preview changes with WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchPersonalWorkspace ytsuda@gmail.com -WhatIf ```
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs without actually making changes.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables the attended robot session for users.

Enables the attended robot session (MayHaveRobotSession) for specified users in UiPath Orchestrator. This is a convenience wrapper around Update-OrchUser that sets -MayHaveRobotSession to True for the specified users.
When a user has MayHaveRobotSession enabled, they are allowed to run attended automations using UiPath Assistant on their machine.
The -UserName parameter supports wildcards to enable the attended session for multiple users at once. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
The -UserName and -Path parameters support tab completion. Press [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab] to see available values.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users, PUT /odata/Users({userId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users
Required permissions: Users.View, Users.Edit
Specifies the target Orch: drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Tab completion suggests available Orch: drives.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the usernames of users to enable the attended robot session for. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Specifies the target Orch: drives. If not specified, the current drive is targeted. Tab completion suggests available Orch: drives.
Specifies the usernames of users to enable the attended robot session for. This is a mandatory parameter. Supports wildcards. Tab completion dynamically suggests usernames from the target tenant.
Runs the command in a mode that only reports what would happen without performing the actions.
You can pipe a single username to this cmdlet via the UserName property.
You can pipe usernames and path values to this cmdlet via the UserName and Path properties.
This cmdlet does not produce output. The user is updated via Update-OrchUser internally.

maml:alert.maml:para:
This is a PowerShell script function that wraps Update-OrchUser with -MayHaveRobotSession True. Each specified user is updated individually in a loop.
Users are tenant-scoped entities. Navigate to the root of an Orch: drive or use -Path to specify target drives.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Enable attended robot for a specific user ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda@gmail.com ```
Enables the attended robot session for the user "ytsuda@gmail.com". The -UserName parameter is positional (position 0) so the parameter name can be omitted.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Enable attended robot for multiple users with a wildcard ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda* ```
Enables the attended robot session for all users whose username starts with "ytsuda".

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Enable attended robot from a specific drive ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Enable-OrchUserAttended -Path Orch1:\ -UserName ytsuda+c@gmail.com ```
Enables the attended robot session for "ytsuda+c@gmail.com" on the Orch1 tenant. When -Path uses an absolute path, the command can be run from any location.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 4: Preview changes with -WhatIf ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Enable-OrchUserAttended ytsuda* -WhatIf ```
Shows which users would have their attended robot session enabled without actually making changes.

maml:description.maml:para: Finds folders that have no user assignments.

Recursively checks all folders under a specified path and returns folders where no users are assigned. By default, only direct user assignments are considered. Users inherited from parent folders are not counted unless the -IncludeInherited switch is specified.
This cmdlet is useful for identifying folders that may need user assignments or that can be cleaned up.
This is a script function (external help file: UiPathOrch-Help.xml) that uses Get-ChildItem (dir) with -Recurse to enumerate folders, and Get-OrchFolderUser to check user assignments for each folder.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetUsersForFolder(key={folderId},includeInherited={bool})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: SubFolders.View, Users.View
Specifies the path to the folder where the search should start.

When specified, the cmdlet considers users inherited from parent folders as part of the user assignments. Without this switch, only direct user assignments are checked. A folder with only inherited users (no direct assignments) is returned when this switch is not used, but excluded when this switch is used.

When specified, the cmdlet considers users inherited from parent folders as part of the user assignments. Without this switch, only direct user assignments are checked. A folder with only inherited users (no direct assignments) is returned when this switch is not used, but excluded when this switch is used.
Specifies the path to the folder where the search should start.
Returns Folder objects for each folder that has no user assignments.

maml:alert.maml:para: By default, users inherited from parent folders are ignored. If the -IncludeInherited switch is provided, inherited users will be included in the check.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Find folders without direct user assignments ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned Orch1:\ ```
Recursively checks all folders under the Orch1:\ root and lists those without direct user assignments.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Find folders without any user assignments including inherited ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned Orch1:\ -IncludeInherited ```
Recursively checks all folders under the Orch1:\ root and lists those without any user assignments, including users inherited from parent folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets jobs that have video recordings attached.

Gets jobs that have video recordings from UiPath Orchestrator. This function filters jobs from the last week that have the HasVideoRecorded property set to true, returning only process-type jobs with attached video recordings.
This is a convenience wrapper around Get-OrchJob that applies the following filters: -ProcessType Process, -Last Week, and HasVideoRecorded -eq $true.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Jobs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Gets only the specified number of jobs. Enter the number of jobs to get.
Skips the specified number of jobs and then gets the remaining jobs. Enter the number of jobs to skip.
Gets only the specified number of jobs. Enter the number of jobs to get.
Specifies the target folders. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Skips the specified number of jobs and then gets the remaining jobs. Enter the number of jobs to skip.
You can pipe folder paths to this cmdlet.
Returns Job objects that have video recordings attached, with properties including HasVideoRecorded set to true.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a PowerShell function that wraps Get-OrchJob with the filters -ProcessType Process, -Last Week, and HasVideoRecorded -eq $true.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Get jobs with video recordings ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobVideo ```
Gets all jobs with video recordings from the last week in the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Get jobs with video recordings recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobVideo -Recurse ```
Gets all jobs with video recordings from the last week across all folders.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Get the first 10 jobs with video recordings ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJobVideo -First 10 ```
Gets the first 10 jobs with video recordings from the last week in the current folder.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test data queue items and displays them in a formatted table.

Gets items from test data queues and displays them in a formatted table view. This function is a wrapper around Get-OrchTestDataQueueItem that expands the JSON content of each item into individual columns, making it easier to view structured test data.
The output is grouped by test data queue path. Each group displays the queue name followed by a table showing the Id, IsConsumed status, and all data columns parsed from the JSON content.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder is targeted.
Includes the target folder and all its subfolders in the operation.
You can pipe a folder path to this cmdlet.
This cmdlet outputs formatted table data to the console. Each test data queue is displayed with its items in a table that includes Id, IsConsumed, and all columns parsed from the JSON content.

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a PowerShell function that wraps Get-OrchTestDataQueueItem and formats the output using Format-Table for improved readability.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 1: Display all test data queue items as a table ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\root> Get-OrchTestDataQueueItemTable ```
Gets all test data queue items from the current folder and displays them in a formatted table grouped by queue.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 2: Display test data queue items from a specific folder ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS C:\> Get-OrchTestDataQueueItemTable Orch1:\root ```
Gets all test data queue items from the root folder on Orch1 and displays them in a formatted table.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 3: Display test data queue items recursively ---------

maml:introduction.maml:para: ```powershell PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchTestDataQueueItemTable -Recurse ```
Gets all test data queue items from the current folder and all its subfolders, displaying them in formatted tables grouped by queue.
